title: Job Openings at Dragnet Solutions Limited
content:
Dragnet Solutions Limited is a firm that specializes in the design, development and implementation of people screening solutions that can be deployed in a variety of uses covering both the workplace and the educational arena.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Shipping and Admiralty Lawyer

Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria
Practice Area: Shipping, Maritime, and Admiralty Law
Employment Type: Full-Time

Job Summary

Our client seeking an experienced and commercially astute Shipping and Admiralty Lawyer with at least 5 years of post-call experience.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The successful candidate will have in-depth knowledge of maritime law and demonstrated experience advising clients in shipping, admiralty claims, marine insurance, and regulatory compliance.
	This is a unique opportunity to work on high-profile maritime matters involving both domestic and international clients.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide legal advice on shipping, maritime, and admiralty matters including vessel arrests, collisions, salvage, and cargo claims.
	Draft, review, and negotiate charterparties, bills of lading, marine insurance contracts, and shipping finance agreements.
	Represent clients in maritime litigation, arbitration, and regulatory proceedings.
	Advise on compliance with Nigerian and international maritime regulations, including NIMASA, IMO, MARPOL, and UNCLOS.
	Handle marine disputes involving shipowners, charterers, P&I Clubs, port authorities, and insurers.
	Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams on cross-border shipping and trade-related transactions.
	Mentor junior associates and contribute to knowledge sharing within the shipping/admiralty practice group.


Requirements


	Qualified to practice law in Nigeria with a minimum of 5 years post-call experience in shipping and maritime law.
	Strong litigation and dispute resolution background in admiralty-related matters.
	Excellent drafting and negotiation skills with a focus on maritime contracts and regulatory compliance.
	Familiarity with international conventions and local shipping laws and regulations.
	Ability to manage complex legal matters and communicate effectively with clients and stakeholders.
	Commercially minded with strong research and analytical capabilities.


Preferred Qualifications:


	LL.M in Maritime Law or relevant certification in shipping law.
	Membership of maritime associations such as the Nigerian Maritime Law Association or IBA Maritime Committee.
	Experience working with port authorities, shipping lines, or marine insurers is an added advantage.


Application Closing Date: 31st March, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

2.) Senior Transactional Lawyer

Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria
Practice Area: Corporate/Commercial Law – Energy & Financial Transactions
Employment Type: Full-Time

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a highly skilled Transactional Lawyer with over 5 years of post-call experience to join its dynamic team.
	The ideal candidate will have demonstrable experience advising on complex energy and financial transactions, including project finance, capital raising, joint ventures, and regulatory compliance.
	This role offers the opportunity to work on high-impact deals within Nigeria’s energy and financial sectors, while providing strategic legal advice to top-tier local and international clients.


Key Responsibilities


	Advise clients on corporate and commercial transactions within the oil & gas, power, and financial services sectors.
	Structure, negotiate, and draft key transaction documents, including Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), Joint Venture Agreements, Loan Agreements, Shareholder Agreements, Investment Agreements, and Security Documents.
	Conduct and lead legal due diligence for M&A and project finance transactions.
	Advise on compliance with Nigerian laws and regulations, including those enforced by DPR/NUPRC, NERC, CBN, SEC, and NAICOM.
	Provide regulatory and advisory support on cross-border investments, corporate structuring, and repatriation of capital.
	Interface with regulators, clients, financiers, and counterparties throughout the transaction lifecycle.
	Supervise junior associates and contribute to knowledge development within the firm.


Requirements


	Qualified to practice law in Nigeria; minimum 5 years post-call experience in a top-tier law firm or corporate legal department.
	Significant experience advising on energy transactions (oil & gas, renewables, or power) and/or financial transactions (project finance, capital markets, private equity).
	Strong command of Nigerian corporate law, project structuring, and regulatory frameworks.
	Proven ability to manage high-value transactions with limited supervision.
	Excellent drafting, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Strong commercial awareness and ability to tailor legal advice to business needs.


Desirable:


	Experience with cross-border financing, syndications, or export credit agency (ECA) transactions.
	Familiarity with industry-specific frameworks such as PIA, Electric Power Sector Reform Act, BOFIA, and Investment and Securities Act.
	A relevant post-graduate qualification (LL.M., MBA) is an advantage.


Application Closing Date: 31st March, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

3.) Chief Business Development Officer

Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria
Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Job Description


	We are seeking an experienced Chief Business Development Officer (CBDO) to lead revenue growth, commercial strategy, and business expansion.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute commercial and growth strategies
	Drive client acquisition and partnership development
	Lead subscription strategy and revenue optimisation
	Oversee digital marketing and lead generation
	Manage CRM systems and performance analytics
	Strengthen brand visibility and market positioning


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Marketing, Economics, or related field
	MBA preferred, Postgraduate
	Minimum of 5 years of leadership experience in Business development, Digital marketing, Subscription-based business models
	Proven track record of achieving revenue targets
	Strong understanding of CRM and digital marketing tools


Preferred Criteria:


	Experience in financial services, media, or research organisations
	Candidate should not be above 45 years old.


Compensation
Competitive salary plus performance-based incentives.

Application Closing Date: 8th March, 2026.

Click here to apply online

     

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Customer Service Executive

Job Locations:&nbsp;Abuja and Lagos

Job Summary


	The Customer Service Executive is responsible for Ticketing and Reservations, check-in, boarding and Baggage services.
	The Customer service executive reports to Station Manager and are assigned the responsibility of exercising efficient and effective sales, check-in, boarding and baggage services of all United Nigeria Airlines Ltd.


Duties and Responsibilities


	Ensures Tickets are not sold on credit Adheres strictly to all Ticketing Time Limit requirements established by United Nigeria.
	For all Ticket purchases - all payments must be in cash or credit cards before ticket is issued
	Ensures update with relevant information regording flight schedules, Flight timing and fares

	Ensures receipts, tickets, ticket jackets are available at all time if not make timely request to the supervisor
	Ensures daily flight program is readily available
	Ensures check-in, sales, boarding of passengers are carried out according to United Nigeria SOP
	Ensures sales, excess baggage charge reconciliation at the end of daily&#39;s operations
	Ensures intending passengers hold valid ticket before check-in and boarding.
	Ensures valid identification before check-in
	Generates passenger manifest and make sure it goes with the flight
	Attends to any other duty assigned by the supervisor.


Education and Experiences


	HND or B.Sc in any discipline
	A higher degree will be an added advantage.
	Must be able to work late hours and multi task.
	A minimum of one (1) year experience in relevant field


Required Skills/Ability:


	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Excellent interpersonal and customer service skills.
	Excellent sales and customer service skills.
	Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
	Excellent time management skills with a proven ability to meet deadlines.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

	Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite or related software.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Resume and Cover Letter detailing their relevant experience, Location and qualifications&nbsp;to:&nbsp;careers@flyunitednigeria.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Executive Job at United Nigeria Airlines




        
    

        
        United Nigeria Airlines is a wholly owned Nigerian company incorporated under the Companies and Allied Matters Act of 1990 at the Corporate Affairs Commission to offer commercial air transportation services under the trade name United Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive

Job Locations: Abuja and Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Customer Service Executive is responsible for Ticketing and Reservations, check-in, boarding and Baggage services.
	The Customer service executive reports to Station Manager and are assigned the responsibility of exercising efficient and effective sales, check-in, boarding and baggage services of all United Nigeria Airlines Ltd.


Duties and Responsibilities


	Ensures Tickets are not sold on credit Adheres strictly to all Ticketing Time Limit requirements established by United Nigeria.
	For all Ticket purchases - all payments must be in cash or credit cards before ticket is issued
	Ensures update with relevant information regording flight schedules, Flight timing and fares
	Ensures all necessary tools/materials needed for reservation and ticketing, check-in, Boarding, are available and ready for use before commencement of duty
	Ensures receipts, tickets, ticket jackets are available at all time if not make timely request to the supervisor
	Ensures daily flight program is readily available
	Ensures check-in, sales, boarding of passengers are carried out according to United Nigeria SOP
	Ensures sales, excess baggage charge reconciliation at the end of daily's operations
	Ensures intending passengers hold valid ticket before check-in and boarding.
	Ensures valid identification before check-in
	Generates passenger manifest and make sure it goes with the flight
	Attends to any other duty assigned by the supervisor.


Education and Experiences


	HND or B.Sc in any discipline
	A higher degree will be an added advantage.
	Must be able to work late hours and multi task.
	A minimum of one (1) year experience in relevant field


Required Skills/Ability:


	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Excellent interpersonal and customer service skills.
	Excellent sales and customer service skills.
	Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
	Excellent time management skills with a proven ability to meet deadlines.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Strong supervisory and leadership skills.
	Ability to develop working partnerships with all staff in airlines at the airport
	Ability to prioritize tasks and to delegate them when appropriate.
	Ability to function well in a high-paced and at times stressful environment.
	Must be smart enough to take critical decisions within a very short time limit
	Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite or related software.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Resume and Cover Letter detailing their relevant experience, Location and qualifications to: careers@flyunitednigeria.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Ellasot Consulting - Our client, a reputable&nbsp;Oil &amp; Gas Servicing company, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer (Graduate Trainee)

Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	This role is ideal for&nbsp;early-career professionalslooking to build a strong career in procurement and supply chain management within the oil and gas industry.
	The successful candidate will gain hands-on experience in vendor management, sourcing strategy, and procurement operations while working in a fast-paced professional environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare procurement proposals, RFQs, and support purchase negotiations
	Source and evaluate reliable vendors for materials, equipment, and services
	Review, compare, and analyze supplier quotations and recommendations
	Monitor supplier performance and resolve procurement-related issues
	Inspect and evaluate purchased items to ensure quality standards
	Support sourcing strategies through market and industry trend analysis
	Maintain strong vendor relationships and assist in contract negotiations
	Conduct supplier research and maintain procurement records


Requirements


	B.Sc Degree / HND in Supply Chain Management or a related discipline with&nbsp;0&ndash;2 years post-NYSC experience (Graduate Trainees encouraged to apply)
	Minimum of 1 year of exposure to foreign or local procurement within an oil &amp; gas servicing environment (internship experience may be considered)
	Knowledge of oilfield equipment, materials, and service procurement processes
	Familiarity with NIPEX and Nigerian Content requirements is an added advantage
	Strong analytical and data management skills
	High attention to detail and willingness to learn
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, professional environment


What You&rsquo;ll Gain


	Hands-on industry procurement experience
	Exposure to oil &amp; gas supply chain operations
	Structured learning and professional development
	Opportunity to grow into advanced procurement roles


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to:&nbsp;frontdeskellasot@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Procurement Officer (Graduate Trainee) Job at Ellasot Consulting




        
    

        
        Ellasot Consulting - Our client, a reputable Oil & Gas Servicing company, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer (Graduate Trainee)

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This role is ideal for early-career professionalslooking to build a strong career in procurement and supply chain management within the oil and gas industry.
	The successful candidate will gain hands-on experience in vendor management, sourcing strategy, and procurement operations while working in a fast-paced professional environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare procurement proposals, RFQs, and support purchase negotiations
	Source and evaluate reliable vendors for materials, equipment, and services
	Review, compare, and analyze supplier quotations and recommendations
	Monitor supplier performance and resolve procurement-related issues
	Inspect and evaluate purchased items to ensure quality standards
	Support sourcing strategies through market and industry trend analysis
	Maintain strong vendor relationships and assist in contract negotiations
	Conduct supplier research and maintain procurement records


Requirements


	B.Sc Degree / HND in Supply Chain Management or a related discipline with 0–2 years post-NYSC experience (Graduate Trainees encouraged to apply)
	Minimum of 1 year of exposure to foreign or local procurement within an oil & gas servicing environment (internship experience may be considered)
	Knowledge of oilfield equipment, materials, and service procurement processes
	Familiarity with NIPEX and Nigerian Content requirements is an added advantage
	Strong analytical and data management skills
	High attention to detail and willingness to learn
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, professional environment


What You’ll Gain


	Hands-on industry procurement experience
	Exposure to oil & gas supply chain operations
	Structured learning and professional development
	Opportunity to grow into advanced procurement roles


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: frontdeskellasot@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
